extractions.problem: Why do unicellular organisms shift to energetically inefficient metabolism at higher growth rates, and how do cell size and ribosomal content co-vary with growth rate?
extractions.method: Mathematical models of self-replicating systems integrating metabolism, ribosomes, and enzyme synthesis, with growth rate maximization as the optimization objective.
extractions.finding: The shift in metabolic efficiency originates from a tradeoff between investments in enzyme synthesis and metabolic yields for alternative catabolic pathways; these patterns emerge from first principles when optimizing the entire self-replicating system rather than individual subsystems.
extractions.baseline: Models considering only partial physiology (e.g., metabolism alone)

extractions.problem: Modeling intracellular processes that depend on spatial locations and stochastic collisions of individual protein molecules requires accurate and efficient spatial simulation tools.
extractions.method: Smoldyn 2.1, a particle-based simulator using Smoluchowski-framework algorithms for diffusion, membrane interactions, and reactions of individual molecules in 3D space.
extractions.finding: Smoldyn was more accurate and computationally efficient than MCell and ChemCell; a yeast pheromone model showed that secreted Bar1 protease sharpens the concentration gradient to help identify the fittest mating partner.
extractions.dataset: Yeast pheromone response system (~200,000 molecules, ~7,000 cubic microns)
extractions.result: 75 min simulated time in ~10 hours runtime

extractions.problem: Previous flux balance analyses addressed metabolism or metabolism-regulation coupling independently, without accounting for extracellular signaling cues and their interplay with metabolic and regulatory networks.
extractions.method: Integrated dynamic FBA (idFBA) combining stoichiometric reconstructions of signaling, metabolic, and transcriptional regulatory networks with quasi-steady-state assumptions and time-delayed inclusion of slow reactions.
extractions.finding: idFBA produced dynamic profiles of biomass and substrate consumption comparable to an equivalent kinetic model, while requiring only linear programming and no exhaustive kinetic parameterization.
extractions.dataset: Prototypic integrated system; S. cerevisiae osmoregulation module
extractions.metric: Dynamic profiles of biomass production, substrate consumption
extractions.baseline: Equivalent detailed kinetic model of the same system
extractions.result: Comparable time-course predictions to kinetic model

extractions.problem: Determining differential expression in replicated count data from digital gene expression technologies, accounting for both biological and technical variability.
extractions.method: edgeR uses an overdispersed Poisson (negative binomial) model with empirical Bayes moderation of dispersion across transcripts to improve inference reliability.
extractions.finding: edgeR enables differential expression analysis even with minimal replication (one or two replicates per condition), and is applicable beyond sequencing to other count data such as proteome peptide counts.
extractions.dataset: Digital gene expression (DGE) / RNA-Seq count data

extractions.problem: A pneumonia epidemic of unknown etiology emerged in Wuhan, China in December 2019, requiring identification and characterization of the causative pathogen.
extractions.method: Full-length genome sequencing from patient samples, phylogenetic analysis, virus isolation on Vero E6 and Huh7 cells, receptor binding studies, and serum neutralization assays.
extractions.finding: The novel coronavirus (2019-nCoV/SARS-CoV-2) shares 79.6% sequence identity with SARS-CoV and 96% with bat coronavirus RaTG13, and uses the ACE2 receptor for cell entry; patient sera could neutralize the isolated virus.
extractions.dataset: 5 patient BALF/swab samples, bat coronavirus RaTG13, 2,794 confirmed cases
extractions.result: 79.6% identity to SARS-CoV, 96% to bat coronavirus RaTG13

extractions.problem: Inferring differential signal in high-throughput sequencing count data requires proper estimation of data variability across the dynamic range and a suitable error model.
extractions.method: DESeq models count data with a negative binomial distribution, linking variance and mean via local regression, with variance estimates pooled across genes of similar expression strength.
extractions.finding: The method provides robust differential expression analysis for count data with few replicates by sharing information across genes for variance estimation.

extractions.problem: Simultaneous measurement of multiple modalities in single cells necessitates computational methods that can integrate data types of varying quality to define cellular states.
extractions.method: Weighted-nearest neighbor (WNN) analysis, an unsupervised framework that learns cell-specific modality weights to generate an integrated multimodal representation; applied within Seurat v4.
extractions.finding: Integrative multimodal analysis substantially improves cell state resolution compared to unimodal analysis, identifying previously unreported lymphoid subpopulations and enabling rapid mapping of new datasets including vaccination and COVID-19 immune responses.
extractions.dataset: CITE-seq human PBMCs with 228 antibodies (hundreds of thousands of cells), cord blood 8,617 cells
extractions.baseline: Independent unimodal analysis of RNA or protein separately
extractions.result: Improved cell state resolution over unimodal analysis

extractions.problem: Translating cancer genomic diversity into therapeutic predictions requires large-scale preclinical models with detailed genetic and pharmacological annotation.
extractions.method: Cancer Cell Line Encyclopedia (CCLE): gene expression, copy number, and massively parallel sequencing of 947 cell lines combined with 8-point dose-response pharmacological profiling for 24 drugs across 479 lines; predictive modeling via naive Bayes and elastic net regression.
extractions.finding: Identified genetic, lineage, and expression-based predictors of drug sensitivity, including novel associations: AHR expression with MEK inhibitor efficacy in NRAS-mutant lines, SLFN11 with topoisomerase inhibitor sensitivity, and plasma cell lineage with IGF1R inhibitor response.
extractions.dataset: 947 human cancer cell lines, 24 anticancer drugs

extractions.problem: High-throughput single-cell RNA sequencing does not capture phenotypic information such as cell-surface protein levels.
extractions.method: CITE-seq: oligonucleotide-labeled antibodies enable simultaneous measurement of surface protein and transcriptome in single cells, leveraging DNA-dependent DNA polymerase activity of reverse transcriptase.
extractions.finding: CITE-seq successfully integrates cellular protein and transcriptome measurements into a single-cell readout, is compatible with existing scRNA-seq approaches, and scales with throughput increases.
extractions.dataset: Single cells with surface protein markers

extractions.problem: The fundamental mechanisms generating cell-to-cell variability from identical DNA sequences remain elusive, limiting understanding of developmental plasticity and tumor heterogeneity.
extractions.method: Single-cell ATAC-seq (scATAC-seq) integrated into a programmable microfluidics platform to map the accessible genome of individual cells across multiple cell types.
extractions.finding: Accessibility variance is systematically associated with specific trans-factors and cis-elements; combinations of trans-factors either induce or suppress variability; single-cell accessibility patterns recapitulate chromosome compartments de novo.
extractions.dataset: Hundreds of single cells across 8 mammalian cell types
extractions.baseline: Bulk ATAC-seq (tens of millions of cells)
extractions.result: Single-cell aggregate maps closely resemble bulk accessibility profiles
